channel.title: Bali on Bali Kini
channel.description: Recent content in Bali on Bali Kini

item.title: Balinale 2026: 94 Film dari 38 Negara Meriahkan Sanur
item.description:
Sanur, Bali, kini menjadi pusat perhatian dunia perfilman internasional. Festival Film Internasional Bali, yang lebih dikenal dengan nama Balinale, memasuki edisi ke-19 pada 1–7 Juni 2026. Sebanyak 94 film dari 38 negara ditayangkan di berbagai venue di kawasan Sanur, menjadikan Balinale sebagai salah satu festival film bergengsi di Asia Tenggara.

Tema dan Semangat Edisi Ke-19

Mengusung tema “Stories Worth Experiencing” (Cerita yang Layak untuk Dialami), Balinale 2026 hadir dengan semangat merayakan kekuatan sinema sebagai medium penceritaan universal. Edisi tahun ini terasa istimewa karena Balinale telah resmi diakui sebagai festival film berkualifikasi Academy Award® (Oscar) — satu-satunya di Indonesia — menjadikan setiap penghargaan film pendek di festival ini memiliki bobot lebih dalam industri perfilman global.

item.title: Balinale 2026: 94 Films from 38 Countries Illuminate Sanur
item.description:
Sanur, Bali has become the center of the international film world’s attention. The Bali International Film Festival, widely known as Balinale, is celebrating its 19th edition from June 1–7, 2026. Ninety-four films from 38 countries are being screened across multiple venues in the Sanur area, cementing Balinale’s position as one of Southeast Asia’s most prestigious film festivals.

Theme and Spirit of the 19th Edition

Carrying the theme “Stories Worth Experiencing”, Balinale 2026 arrives with a spirit of celebrating the power of cinema as a universal storytelling medium. This edition is particularly significant: Balinale has been officially recognized as an Academy Award®-qualifying film festival — the only one in Indonesia — meaning every short film award carries real weight in the global film industry.

item.title: BPS Bali Finds Anomaly: Foreign Tourists Down 6%, Hotels Fuller Than Ever
item.description:
Bali’s Central Statistics Agency (BPS) has flagged an unusual phenomenon in the island’s tourism landscape for April 2026: the number of foreign tourist (wisman) arrivals fell on a year-on-year basis, yet hotel room occupancy rates rose. The anomaly raises new questions about the quality-versus-quantity debate at the heart of Bali’s tourism strategy.

April 2026 Tourist Data: Monthly Rebound, Annual Decline

According to the official BPS Bali release, foreign tourists arriving directly in Bali in April 2026 totalled 553,328 visits. Month-on-month, that figure rose 17.21 percent compared to March 2026 — a seasonal rebound typical of the lead-up to the Northern Hemisphere summer.
